gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/47)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/47)

parts.text: بَعْضِ أَصْحَابِ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) مِمَّنْ يُوثَقُ بِهِ أَنَّ أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) تَكَلَّمَ بِهَذَا الْكَلَامِ
خَطَبَ بِهِ عَلَى مِنْبَرِ الْكُوفَةِ اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّهُ لَا بُدَّ لَكَ مِنْ حُجَجٍ

text: أَرْضِكَ حُجَّةٍ بَعْدَ حُجَّةٍ عَلَى خَلْقِكَ يَهْدُونَهُمْ إِلَى دِينِكَ وَ يُعَلِّمُونَهُمْ عِلْمَكَ كَيْلَا يَتَفَرَّقَ

أَتْبَاعُ أَوْلِيَائِكَ ظَاهِرٍ غَيْرِ مُطَاعٍ أَوْ مُكْتَتَمٍ يُتَرَقَّبُ إِنْ غَابَ عَنِ النَّاسِ شَخْصُهُمْ فِي حَالِ هُدْنَتِهِمْ فَلَمْ يَغِبْ عَنْهُمْ قَدِيمُ مَبْثُوثِ عِلْمِهِمْ وَ آدَابُهُمْ فِي قُلُوبِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ مُثْبَتَةٌ فَهُمْ بِهَا عَامِلُونَ

وَ يَقُولُ ( عليه السلام ) فِي هَذِهِ الْخُطْبَةِ فِي مَوْضِعٍ آخَرَ فِيمَنْ هَذَا وَ لِهَذَا يَأْرِزُ الْعِلْمُ إِذَا لَمْ يُوجَدْ لَهُ حَمَلَةٌ يَحْفَظُونَهُ وَ يَرْوُونَهُ كَمَا سَمِعُوهُ مِنَ الْعُلَمَاءِ وَ يَصْدُقُونَ عَلَيْهِمْ فِيهِ

اللَّهُمَّ فَإِنِّي لَأَعْلَمُ أَنَّ الْعِلْمَ لَا يَأْرِزُ كُلُّهُ وَ لَا يَنْقَطِعُ مَوَادُّهُ وَ إِنَّكَ لَا تُخْلِي أَرْضَكَ مِنْ حُجَّةٍ لَكَ عَلَى خَلْقِكَ ظَاهِرٍ لَيْسَ بِالْمُطَاعِ أَوْ خَائِفٍ مَغْمُورٍ كَيْلَا تَبْطُلَ حُجَّتُكَ وَ لَا يَضِلَّ أَوْلِيَاؤُكَ بَعْدَ إِذْ هَدَيْتَهُمْ بَلْ أَيْنَ هُمْ وَ كَمْ هُمْ أُولَئِكَ الْأَقَلُّونَ عَدَداً الْأَعْظَمُونَ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ قَدْراً .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Muhammad, from Sahl Bin Ziyad and Muhammad Bin Yahya and someone else, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad and Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father altogether, from Ibn Mahboub, from Hisham Bin Salim, from Abu Hamza, from Abu Is’haq Al Sabai’e,

(It has been narrated) from one of the companions of Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup>, from the one who was reliable with it that Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> spoke with this speech, and he Memorised it from him<sup>asws</sup>, and he<sup>asws</sup> addressed with it upon the Pulpit of Al-Kufa: ‘O Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! It is Inevitable for You<sup>azwj</sup> that there be a Divine Authority for You<sup>azwj</sup> in Your<sup>azwj</sup> earth, a Divine Authority after a Divine Authority upon Your<sup>azwj</sup> creatures, guiding them towards Your<sup>azwj</sup> Religion and teaching them Your<sup>azwj</sup> Knowledge lest they scatter.

The followers of Your<sup>azwj</sup> Guardians<sup>asws</sup> in the apparent are without obedience, or in concealment, and they<sup>asws</sup> would be awaited. If their<sup>asws</sup> persons are absent from the people during a state of their<sup>asws</sup> truce, but their<sup>asws</sup> ancient knowledge and their<sup>asws</sup> education would be spread out in the hearts of the Momineen, affirmed. Thus, they would be acting with it’.

And he<sup>asws</sup> said during this sermon in another place: ‘So in who would this be? And due to this the knowledge is obscured when a bearer cannot be found for it who would be preserving it and reporting it just as he had heard it from the knowledgeable ones<sup>asws</sup>, and he would be truthful upon them with regards to it.

O Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! So I<sup>asws</sup> know that the knowledge, all of it cannot be obscured, nor would its items be cut-off, and You<sup>azwj</sup> do not Leave Your<sup>azwj</sup> earth empty from a Divine Authority of Yours<sup>saww</sup> upon Your<sup>azwj</sup> creatures. He<sup>asws</sup> would either be apparent and wouldn’t be with a following or fearful obscured, lest Your<sup>azwj</sup> Divine Authority be invalidated, and You<sup>azwj</sup> do not Let Your<sup>azwj</sup> friends to stray after having Guided them. But where are they, and how many are Your<sup>azwj</sup> friends. There are few in number, magnified in the Presence of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> in worth’.
en.sarwar: 13. Ali ibn Muhammad has narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad and Muhammad ibn Yahya and others from Ahmad ibn Muhammad and Ali ibn Ibrahim from his father, all three of them from ibn Mahbub from Hisham ibn Salim from abu Hamza from abu Ishaq al-Sabi’Isma‘il from a reliable one of the companions of Amir al-Mu’minin (a.s.) who has said the following. “Amir al-Mu’minin (a.s.) once spoke the words herein below from the pulpit in Kufa and I memorized them. “O Lord it is certain that You must have a person with Your Authority on Yaour earth. Such persons would come one after the other whith Your authority over the creatures who would guide people in your religion and teach them your knowledge so that your friends would not scatter. They can be in the public sight but disobeyed or out of public sight whose reappearance would be expected. Even though their persons will be hidden form the people in peace time but their knowledge that has spread before will not be hidden from the people. Their discipline will firmly be established in the hearts of people and they will act up on them.” In another part of this sermon he has (a.s.) said, “Who would it be to have such discipline?” For this reason knowledge becomes obscure when it does not find any one to bear, protect and narrate it to others just as they have heard it from the scholars and speak truthfully from them in it. O Lord, I know that all the knowledge will not become obscure and all of its sources will not banish. You will not leave your earth without a person with Your authority over Your. He may either live in public sight but is not obeyed and followed or is afraid and hidden from public sight so that Your authority is not invalidated and Your friends are not mislead after You have granted them guidance. In fact, where are they and how many are they? They are very few in number but of very great honor and respect before Allah.”
